note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. sec-fix REMEDIATES a specific security finding — minimal tested patch + regression test — downstream of the sec-* scanners that only DETECT. Negatives are near-misses that share security/fix vocabulary but belong to a detection sibling (sec-scan, sec-secrets, sec-deps, sec-iac, sec-gate, sec-ci, sec-compliance), to debug (functional bug), to task-fix-pr (PR review comments), to refactor-apply (refactor rails), or to a plain commit/edit action.

queries.text: Semgrep flagged a SQL injection in src/api/users.ts:42 — patch it and add a regression test
fix this vulnerability: the login route builds the query with string interpolation on the email param
remediate the CVE Trivy found in our base image — give me the minimal diff
close the XSS in the comment renderer, it dumps user input into innerHTML — want the fix plus a test that proves it's closed
just ran sec-scan and it found a hardcoded API key in config.py — patch it and tell me how to rotate
the auditor said we're missing an ownership check on GET /orders/:id (IDOR) — write the fix and a regression test
Bandit says we hash passwords with sha256 — swap it for bcrypt with a test, minimal change only
path traversal in the file-download handler lets you read ../../etc/passwd — remediate this finding
we've got a command injection where we shell out with the filename — produce a focused patch and a test that fires the attack
Snyk finding: our CORS is set to '*' with credentials — generate the fix and a verification plan
run a full OWASP Top 10 audit of the whole service before we ship to prod
queries.note: comprehensive detection scan → sec-scan, not remediating a specific finding

queries.text: scan the git history for any leaked API keys or tokens we may have committed
audit package.json for vulnerable dependencies and give me the CVE list
queries.note: dependency CVE discovery → sec-deps; sec-fix acts on one finding after

queries.text: the checkout endpoint returns 500 for some carts — help me figure out why it's crashing
queries.note: functional bug, not a security vulnerability → debug

queries.text: review our Terraform IAM for over-permissioned roles
address the review comments on my PR — a reviewer wants me to validate the input
queries.note: applying PR review feedback → task-fix-pr, even though input validation is security-adjacent

queries.text: quick security sanity-check on my staged diff before I commit
queries.note: fast diff-scoped pre-commit detection gate → sec-gate

queries.text: commit the security patch I just wrote
queries.note: persisting an already-written fix → commit, not generating one

queries.text: check our auth flows against OWASP ASVS L2 and give me a gap analysis
this validateUser function is a tangled mess — extract the checks and clean it up
queries.note: code-health refactor of security code → refactor-apply, not a vuln patch

queries.text: sec-scan found a stored XSS in the profile bio — now patch it with a regression test, don't just re-scan
queries.note: names sec-scan but the ask is to remediate the finding → must beat sec-scan

queries.text: we found a hardcoded DB password in settings.py — write the fix to pull it from env and add the .gitignore entry
queries.note: hardcoded secret vocab overlaps sec-secrets, but the ask is to produce the patch → must beat sec-secrets

queries.text: I think we may have leaked credentials somewhere — scan the whole repo and git history for secrets
queries.note: detection sweep across code+history, no specific finding to patch → sec-secrets wins

queries.text: do a comprehensive security audit of the service and surface every vulnerability
queries.note: broad discovery of all vulns, nothing to remediate yet → sec-scan wins
